title: South Korea Mandates Sustainable Aviation Fuel Blending for International Flights From 2027
content:
South Korea will mandate the blending of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) for all international flights departing the country starting in 2027, beginning at 1% and gradually increasing to 3â€“5% in 2030 and 7â€“10% by 2035. The Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy and the Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport launched the â€œSAF Allianceâ€ and released the blending roadmap to accelerate adoption. Obligated suppliersâ€”refiners and jet fuel importers/exportersâ€”must meet annual SAF blending targets, with flexibility mechanisms allowing up to 20% compliance carryover for three years.

The government will establish bio-jet fuel quality standards by mid-2026 and consider incentives for feedstocks with higher carbon reduction rates. It also plans R&D support, tax credits, policy finance, and the designation of key SAF feedstocks as economic security items. Airlines exceeding SAF requirements could gain extra points for international air traffic rights and benefit from airport fee reductions. These steps position Korea to cut aviation emissions, stimulate SAF production, and secure a leading role in the global low-carbon aviation fuel market.
author: Analysts Account
